About Nuerolynx.

Technology consulting and design for modern connected infrastructure.

Independent consulting for connected facilities.

Nuerolynx is the independent consulting practice of Mathew Dutton, specializing in the planning, design, and coordination of communications and electronic security systems.

Founded on the idea that every modern facility depends on intelligent connections, Nuerolynx brings together technology, infrastructure, and project leadership to help organizations design systems that are reliable, scalable, and built for the future.

From early planning and permitting through deployment and project coordination, Nuerolynx provides practical guidance that bridges the gap between owners, facilities teams, IT departments, and field installers.

Independent Consultant Technology consulting and project leadership.
Division 27 Communications infrastructure.
Division 28 Electronic safety and security systems.
Design to Deployment Planning • Coordination • Project Management

Built for clarity. Designed for execution.

Nuerolynx helps translate business needs into coordinated technology systems, reducing confusion between ownership, facilities, IT, design teams, and field installation.

Services.

Professional consulting, design, permitting support, and project management for technology systems that connect and protect modern facilities.

Technology Consulting

Evaluate current systems, identify gaps, review technology options, and define practical upgrade paths.

System Design

Create structured technology plans for networks, access control, video surveillance, alarms, and infrastructure.

Plans & Permits

Support plan creation and permit coordination for applicable access control, alarm, and low-voltage projects.

Project Management

Coordinate scope, schedule, vendors, field execution, documentation, and closeout from planning to deployment.

Owner Representation

Help owners understand scopes, budgets, vendor proposals, system limitations, and long-term value.

Design Review & Coordination

Review layouts, cabling needs, device placement, network requirements, and coordination between owners, facilities, IT, and installers.

Division 27 & Division 28.

The technology standards behind modern buildings.

Where it comes from

In commercial construction, building systems are commonly organized using the Construction Specifications Institute (CSI) MasterFormat. This framework helps architects, engineers, contractors, facility owners, and technology teams coordinate scopes of work, specifications, drawings, and project responsibilities.

Nuerolynx focuses on Division 27 and Division 28 — the sections that define communications infrastructure and electronic safety and security systems.

Why it matters

Using the Division 27 and Division 28 framework helps technology systems get planned earlier, documented clearly, coordinated properly, and deployed with fewer conflicts between owners, facilities teams, IT departments, contractors, and field installers.

The result is cleaner design, better coordination, stronger budgeting, and infrastructure that is easier to manage after the project is complete.

Division 27

Communications

Division 27 covers the systems that allow buildings, people, and devices to communicate: structured cabling, fiber, networks, wireless, AV, paging, intercom, MDF/IDF spaces, racks, and supporting infrastructure.

Structured Cabling Cat6 / Cat6A Fiber Optics MDF / IDF Design Network Design Wireless Infrastructure AV Systems Paging Intercom UPS Planning
Division 28

Electronic Safety & Security

Division 28 covers the systems that protect people, property, and operations: access control, intrusion detection, video surveillance, visitor management, door hardware integration, identity systems, and cloud-managed security platforms.

Access Control Alarm Systems Video Surveillance Intrusion Detection Visitor Management Door Hardware Integration Cloud Security Platforms System Takeovers

From design intent to deployment.

Nuerolynx bridges the gap between owners, facilities teams, IT personnel, vendors, and field installers to keep technology projects aligned and moving forward.

Assess

Understand existing systems, project goals, pain points, site conditions, and operational needs.

Design

Develop practical system layouts, infrastructure requirements, device planning, and scope direction.

Coordinate

Align owners, IT, facilities, vendors, installers, and project stakeholders throughout execution.

Manage

Support project delivery, documentation, punch work, closeout, future expansion, and system readiness.

Technology experience.

Practical experience across the systems and infrastructure used in commercial technology environments.

Ubiquiti Networks

UniFi gateways, switching, PoE, Wi-Fi, cloud-managed networks, camera infrastructure, and remote access planning.

Security Systems

Access control, alarms, surveillance, credentials, cloud dashboards, door hardware, and integrated security ecosystems.

Infrastructure

Racks, patch panels, pathways, MDF/IDF rooms, UPS power, structured cabling, documentation, and site coordination.

Automation & AI

AI-assisted workflows, automation planning, home lab testing, and future-ready technology development.
